Metadata.Description: 206 Copilot assessment scenarios. Fresh catalog (drops the prior v6.0.0 70-case set). Grounded in live AISupportAnalysisDB values: entity names (Transport, Finance, HR, Health, Education, Legal), statuses, priorities, categories, sources, ticket-number prefixes (TCK-2026-, TCK-AR-2026-). Adds new intent coverage (Similarity, KnowledgeBase, PlatformAction, ExternalTool, Conversational) plus 20 fake/control cases (10 off-topic + 10 mis-labeled) so the suite measures both happy-path accuracy AND router robustness.

Scenarios.Question: Show me all tickets.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.CreatedAt FROM Tickets t ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: List the 10 newest tickets.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 10 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.CreatedAt FROM Tickets t ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Show the 5 oldest tickets.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 5 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.CreatedAt FROM Tickets t ORDER BY t.CreatedAt ASC

Scenarios.Question: Show all tickets from Health.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t JOIN Entitys e ON t.EntityId=e.Id WHERE e.Name='Health' ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Show only unresolved tickets.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t WHERE t.ResolvedAt IS NULL ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: List tickets that have been closed.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.ClosedAt FROM Tickets t WHERE t.ClosedAt IS NOT NULL ORDER BY t.ClosedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Tickets that are still pending.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON t.StatusId=s.Id WHERE s.Name='Pending' ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Give me the top 25 tickets that were updated most recently.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 25 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.UpdatedAt FROM Tickets t ORDER BY t.UpdatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Show tickets that have a parent ticket.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.ParentTicketId FROM Tickets t WHERE t.ParentTicketId IS NOT NULL ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: List Arabic-numbered tickets.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t WHERE t.TicketNumber LIKE 'TCK-AR-%' ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Show all tickets in the Education entity.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t JOIN Entitys e ON t.EntityId=e.Id WHERE e.Name='Education' ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Get me 100 tickets — any 100 will do.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 100 t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Show ticket numbers and titles only.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t

Scenarios.Question: Show ticket number, title, and SLA breach flag.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.IsSlaBreached FROM Tickets t

Scenarios.Question: Show ticket number with status name and priority name.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, s.Name AS StatusName, p.Name AS PriorityName FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON t.StatusId=s.Id JOIN TicketPriorities p ON t.PriorityId=p.Id

Scenarios.Question: Show ticket number, title, entity name, and created date.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, e.Name AS EntityName, t.CreatedAt FROM Tickets t JOIN Entitys e ON t.EntityId=e.Id

Scenarios.Question: Show only ticket number and resolution summary.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.ResolutionSummary FROM Tickets t WHERE t.ResolutionSummary IS NOT NULL

Scenarios.Question: Show ticket number, due date, and resolution due date.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.DueDate, t.ResolutionDueAt FROM Tickets t

Scenarios.Question: Show ticket number with the impact scope and affected user count.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.ImpactScope, t.AffectedUsersCount FROM Tickets t

Scenarios.Question: Show ticket number, browser, operating system, and environment.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.BrowserName, t.OperatingSystem, t.EnvironmentName FROM Tickets t

Scenarios.Question: Show tickets with status Open.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON t.StatusId=s.Id WHERE s.Name='Open' ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Show critical tickets.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketPriorities p ON t.PriorityId=p.Id WHERE p.Name='Critical' ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Show tickets in the Hardware category.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketCategories c ON t.CategoryId=c.Id WHERE c.Name='Hardware' ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Tickets created via Email.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketSources src ON t.SourceId=src.Id WHERE src.Name='Email' ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Tickets where the SLA is breached.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t WHERE t.IsSlaBreached=1 ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Show unassigned tickets.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t WHERE t.AssignedToUserId IS NULL ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Show escalated tickets.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.EscalationLevel FROM Tickets t WHERE t.EscalationLevel IS NOT NULL ORDER BY t.EscalatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Tickets that require manager review.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t WHERE t.RequiresManagerReview=1 ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Show tickets where the affected users count is greater than 10.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.AffectedUsersCount FROM Tickets t WHERE t.AffectedUsersCount > 10 ORDER BY t.AffectedUsersCount DESC

Scenarios.Question: Show tickets with status In Progress.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON t.StatusId=s.Id WHERE s.Name='In Progress' ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Tickets where root cause is not set.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t WHERE t.RootCause IS NULL ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Tickets where the resolution summary is filled in.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.ResolutionSummary FROM Tickets t WHERE t.ResolutionSummary IS NOT NULL ORDER BY t.ResolvedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Tickets in product area Endpoint Health Service.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.ProductArea FROM Tickets t WHERE t.ProductArea='Endpoint Health Service' ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Tickets reported on Windows.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.OperatingSystem FROM Tickets t WHERE t.OperatingSystem LIKE 'Windows%' ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Tickets where the due date is in the past and still unresolved.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.DueDate FROM Tickets t WHERE t.DueDate < GETUTCDATE() AND t.ResolvedAt IS NULL ORDER BY t.DueDate ASC

Scenarios.Question: Tickets created by an Admin user.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t JOIN AspNetUserRoles ur ON t.CreatedByUserId=ur.UserId JOIN AspNetRoles r ON ur.RoleId=r.Id WHERE r.Name='Admin' ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Tickets with affected users between 5 and 50.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.AffectedUsersCount FROM Tickets t WHERE t.AffectedUsersCount BETWEEN 5 AND 50 ORDER BY t.AffectedUsersCount DESC

Scenarios.Question: Tickets with an external reference id set.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.ExternalReferenceId, t.ExternalSystemName FROM Tickets t WHERE t.ExternalReferenceId IS NOT NULL ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Show 20 tickets sorted by creation date descending.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 20 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.CreatedAt FROM Tickets t ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Sort tickets by priority then by creation date.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, p.Name AS PriorityName, t.CreatedAt FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketPriorities p ON t.PriorityId=p.Id ORDER BY p.Level DESC, t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Top 10 tickets by affected users count.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 10 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.AffectedUsersCount FROM Tickets t ORDER BY t.AffectedUsersCount DESC

Scenarios.Question: Show tickets sorted by escalation level (highest first).

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.EscalationLevel FROM Tickets t WHERE t.EscalationLevel IS NOT NULL ORDER BY t.EscalationLevel DESC

Scenarios.Question: Sort tickets by due date ascending.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.DueDate FROM Tickets t WHERE t.DueDate IS NOT NULL ORDER BY t.DueDate ASC

Scenarios.Question: Show oldest unresolved tickets first.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.CreatedAt FROM Tickets t WHERE t.ResolvedAt IS NULL ORDER BY t.CreatedAt ASC

Scenarios.Question: Show tickets with their status name.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, s.Name AS StatusName FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON t.StatusId=s.Id ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Show tickets with their priority name.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, p.Name AS PriorityName FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketPriorities p ON t.PriorityId=p.Id ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Show tickets with their entity name.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, e.Name AS EntityName FROM Tickets t JOIN Entitys e ON t.EntityId=e.Id ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Show tickets with their category name.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, c.Name AS CategoryName FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketCategories c ON t.CategoryId=c.Id ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Show tickets with their source name.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, src.Name AS SourceName FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketSources src ON t.SourceId=src.Id ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Show tickets with the assigned user's full name.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, (u.FirstName+' '+u.LastName) AS AssignedToName FROM Tickets t JOIN AspNetUsers u ON t.AssignedToUserId=u.Id ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Show tickets with the reporter's full name.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, (u.FirstName+' '+u.LastName) AS ReporterName FROM Tickets t JOIN AspNetUsers u ON t.CreatedByUserId=u.Id ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Show tickets with the resolver's full name.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, (u.FirstName+' '+u.LastName) AS ResolvedByName FROM Tickets t JOIN AspNetUsers u ON t.ResolvedByUserId=u.Id ORDER BY t.ResolvedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Show tickets with the entity name and the assigned user's name.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, e.Name AS EntityName, (u.FirstName+' '+u.LastName) AS AssignedToName FROM Tickets t JOIN Entitys e ON t.EntityId=e.Id LEFT JOIN AspNetUsers u ON t.AssignedToUserId=u.Id ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: List tickets with status, priority and entity together.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, s.Name AS StatusName, p.Name AS PriorityName, e.Name AS EntityName FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON t.StatusId=s.Id JOIN TicketPriorities p ON t.PriorityId=p.Id JOIN Entitys e ON t.EntityId=e.Id ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Show resolved tickets with the resolver and the resolution-approver names.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, (r.FirstName+' '+r.LastName) AS ResolvedByName, (a.FirstName+' '+a.LastName) AS ApprovedByName FROM Tickets t LEFT JOIN AspNetUsers r ON t.ResolvedByUserId=r.Id LEFT JOIN AspNetUsers a ON t.ResolutionApprovedByUserId=a.Id WHERE t.ResolvedAt IS NOT NULL ORDER BY t.ResolvedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Show tickets with their parent ticket's number.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, parent.TicketNumber AS ParentTicketNumber FROM Tickets t JOIN Tickets parent ON t.ParentTicketId=parent.Id ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: How many tickets are there in total?

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT COUNT_BIG(1) AS TotalTickets FROM Tickets

Scenarios.Question: Sum of affected users across all tickets.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT SUM(AffectedUsersCount) AS TotalAffectedUsers FROM Tickets

Scenarios.Question: Average affected users per ticket.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT AVG(CAST(AffectedUsersCount AS float)) AS AverageAffectedUsers FROM Tickets

Scenarios.Question: Highest affected users count on any single ticket.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT MAX(AffectedUsersCount) AS MaxAffectedUsers FROM Tickets

Scenarios.Question: Lowest affected users count on any ticket.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT MIN(AffectedUsersCount) AS MinAffectedUsers FROM Tickets

Scenarios.Question: How many distinct entities have tickets?

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT EntityId) AS DistinctEntities FROM Tickets

Scenarios.Question: How many open tickets are there?

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT COUNT_BIG(1) AS OpenCount FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON t.StatusId=s.Id WHERE s.Name='Open'

Scenarios.Question: How many critical tickets are there?

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT COUNT_BIG(1) AS CriticalCount FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketPriorities p ON t.PriorityId=p.Id WHERE p.Name='Critical'

Scenarios.Question: How many tickets breached the SLA?

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT COUNT_BIG(1) AS SlaBreachCount FROM Tickets WHERE IsSlaBreached=1

Scenarios.Question: Average escalation level across escalated tickets.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT AVG(CAST(EscalationLevel AS float)) AS AverageEscalation FROM Tickets WHERE EscalationLevel IS NOT NULL

Scenarios.Question: How many distinct categories are in use?

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT CategoryId) AS DistinctCategories FROM Tickets

Scenarios.Question: How many tickets are unassigned?

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT COUNT_BIG(1) AS UnassignedCount FROM Tickets WHERE AssignedToUserId IS NULL

Scenarios.Question: How many resolved tickets do we have?

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT COUNT_BIG(1) AS ResolvedCount FROM Tickets WHERE ResolvedAt IS NOT NULL

Scenarios.Question: Average ticket resolution time in hours.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT AVG(DATEDIFF(HOUR, CreatedAt, ResolvedAt)*1.0) AS AvgResolutionHours FROM Tickets WHERE ResolvedAt IS NOT NULL

Scenarios.Question: Count of tickets per entity.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT e.Name AS EntityName, COUNT_BIG(1) AS TicketCount FROM Tickets t JOIN Entitys e ON t.EntityId=e.Id GROUP BY e.Name ORDER BY TicketCount DESC

Scenarios.Question: Count of tickets per status.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT s.Name AS StatusName, COUNT_BIG(1) AS TicketCount FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON t.StatusId=s.Id GROUP BY s.Name ORDER BY TicketCount DESC

Scenarios.Question: Count of tickets per priority.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT p.Name AS PriorityName, COUNT_BIG(1) AS TicketCount FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketPriorities p ON t.PriorityId=p.Id GROUP BY p.Name ORDER BY TicketCount DESC

Scenarios.Question: Count of tickets per source.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT src.Name AS SourceName, COUNT_BIG(1) AS TicketCount FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketSources src ON t.SourceId=src.Id GROUP BY src.Name ORDER BY TicketCount DESC

Scenarios.Question: Count of tickets per category.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT c.Name AS CategoryName, COUNT_BIG(1) AS TicketCount FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketCategories c ON t.CategoryId=c.Id GROUP BY c.Name ORDER BY TicketCount DESC

Scenarios.Question: Average affected users by entity.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT e.Name AS EntityName, AVG(CAST(AffectedUsersCount AS float)) AS AverageAffectedUsers FROM Tickets t JOIN Entitys e ON t.EntityId=e.Id GROUP BY e.Name ORDER BY AverageAffectedUsers DESC

Scenarios.Question: Total escalation level by priority.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT p.Name AS PriorityName, SUM(EscalationLevel) AS TotalEscalation FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketPriorities p ON t.PriorityId=p.Id WHERE EscalationLevel IS NOT NULL GROUP BY p.Name ORDER BY TotalEscalation DESC

Scenarios.Question: How many SLA breaches per entity?

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT e.Name AS EntityName, SUM(CASE WHEN IsSlaBreached=1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS SlaBreachCount FROM Tickets t JOIN Entitys e ON t.EntityId=e.Id GROUP BY e.Name ORDER BY SlaBreachCount DESC

Scenarios.Question: Tickets per environment.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT EnvironmentName, COUNT_BIG(1) AS TicketCount FROM Tickets WHERE EnvironmentName IS NOT NULL GROUP BY EnvironmentName ORDER BY TicketCount DESC

Scenarios.Question: Tickets per browser.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT BrowserName, COUNT_BIG(1) AS TicketCount FROM Tickets WHERE BrowserName IS NOT NULL GROUP BY BrowserName ORDER BY TicketCount DESC

Scenarios.Question: Tickets per impact scope.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT ImpactScope, COUNT_BIG(1) AS TicketCount FROM Tickets WHERE ImpactScope IS NOT NULL GROUP BY ImpactScope ORDER BY TicketCount DESC

Scenarios.Question: Tickets per assignee, top 10.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 10 (u.FirstName+' '+u.LastName) AS AssignedToName, COUNT_BIG(1) AS TicketCount FROM Tickets t JOIN AspNetUsers u ON t.AssignedToUserId=u.Id GROUP BY u.FirstName, u.LastName ORDER BY TicketCount DESC

Scenarios.Question: Entities with more than 100 tickets.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT e.Name AS EntityName, COUNT_BIG(1) AS TicketCount FROM Tickets t JOIN Entitys e ON t.EntityId=e.Id GROUP BY e.Name HAVING COUNT_BIG(1)>100 ORDER BY TicketCount DESC

Scenarios.Question: Priorities with more than 300 tickets.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT p.Name AS PriorityName, COUNT_BIG(1) AS TicketCount FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketPriorities p ON t.PriorityId=p.Id GROUP BY p.Name HAVING COUNT_BIG(1)>300 ORDER BY TicketCount DESC

Scenarios.Question: Categories with more than 50 tickets.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT c.Name AS CategoryName, COUNT_BIG(1) AS TicketCount FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketCategories c ON t.CategoryId=c.Id GROUP BY c.Name HAVING COUNT_BIG(1)>50 ORDER BY TicketCount DESC

Scenarios.Question: Statuses with at least 50 tickets each.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT s.Name AS StatusName, COUNT_BIG(1) AS TicketCount FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON t.StatusId=s.Id GROUP BY s.Name HAVING COUNT_BIG(1)>=50 ORDER BY TicketCount DESC

Scenarios.Question: Sources where average affected users is over 5.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT src.Name AS SourceName, AVG(CAST(AffectedUsersCount AS float)) AS AverageAffectedUsers FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketSources src ON t.SourceId=src.Id GROUP BY src.Name HAVING AVG(CAST(AffectedUsersCount AS float))>5 ORDER BY AverageAffectedUsers DESC

Scenarios.Question: Entities where total affected users exceeds 1000.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT e.Name AS EntityName, SUM(AffectedUsersCount) AS TotalAffectedUsers FROM Tickets t JOIN Entitys e ON t.EntityId=e.Id GROUP BY e.Name HAVING SUM(AffectedUsersCount)>1000 ORDER BY TotalAffectedUsers DESC

Scenarios.Question: Tickets created in the last 7 days.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.CreatedAt FROM Tickets t WHERE t.CreatedAt>=DATEADD(day,-7,GETUTCDATE()) ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Tickets created last month.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.CreatedAt FROM Tickets t WHERE t.CreatedAt>=DATEADD(month,-1,DATEFROMPARTS(YEAR(GETUTCDATE()),MONTH(GETUTCDATE()),1)) AND t.CreatedAt<DATEFROMPARTS(YEAR(GETUTCDATE()),MONTH(GETUTCDATE()),1) ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Tickets created this month.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.CreatedAt FROM Tickets t WHERE t.CreatedAt>=DATEFROMPARTS(YEAR(GETUTCDATE()),MONTH(GETUTCDATE()),1) ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Tickets created since 2026-04-01.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.CreatedAt FROM Tickets t WHERE t.CreatedAt>='2026-04-01' ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Tickets resolved in the last 30 days.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.ResolvedAt FROM Tickets t WHERE t.ResolvedAt>=DATEADD(day,-30,GETUTCDATE()) ORDER BY t.ResolvedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Tickets created today.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.CreatedAt FROM Tickets t WHERE CAST(t.CreatedAt AS date)=CAST(GETUTCDATE() AS date) ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Tickets created yesterday.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.CreatedAt FROM Tickets t WHERE CAST(t.CreatedAt AS date)=CAST(DATEADD(day,-1,GETUTCDATE()) AS date) ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Tickets due in the next 7 days.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.DueDate FROM Tickets t WHERE t.DueDate BETWEEN GETUTCDATE() AND DATEADD(day,7,GETUTCDATE()) ORDER BY t.DueDate ASC

Scenarios.Question: Tickets that are overdue.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.DueDate FROM Tickets t WHERE t.DueDate<GETUTCDATE() AND t.ResolvedAt IS NULL ORDER BY t.DueDate ASC

Scenarios.Question: Tickets created between 2026-01-01 and 2026-03-31.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.CreatedAt FROM Tickets t WHERE t.CreatedAt BETWEEN '2026-01-01' AND '2026-03-31 23:59:59' ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Tickets that took more than 48 hours to resolve.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, DATEDIFF(HOUR, t.CreatedAt, t.ResolvedAt) AS ResolutionHours FROM Tickets t WHERE t.ResolvedAt IS NOT NULL AND DATEDIFF(HOUR, t.CreatedAt, t.ResolvedAt)>48 ORDER BY ResolutionHours DESC

Scenarios.Question: Tickets escalated in the last 14 days.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.EscalatedAt, t.EscalationLevel FROM Tickets t WHERE t.EscalatedAt>=DATEADD(day,-14,GETUTCDATE()) ORDER BY t.EscalatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Show details of ticket 1.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT t.* FROM Tickets t WHERE t.Id=1

Scenarios.Question: Show details of ticket TCK-2026-00500.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT t.* FROM Tickets t WHERE t.TicketNumber='TCK-2026-00500'

Scenarios.Question: Show details of ticket TCK-AR-2026-00150.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT t.* FROM Tickets t WHERE t.TicketNumber='TCK-AR-2026-00150'

Scenarios.Question: What is the status of ticket TCK-2026-00100?

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT t.TicketNumber, s.Name AS StatusName FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON t.StatusId=s.Id WHERE t.TicketNumber='TCK-2026-00100'

Scenarios.Question: Who is assigned to ticket TCK-2026-00050?

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT t.TicketNumber, (u.FirstName+' '+u.LastName) AS AssignedToName FROM Tickets t LEFT JOIN AspNetUsers u ON t.AssignedToUserId=u.Id WHERE t.TicketNumber='TCK-2026-00050'

Scenarios.Question: Show me the resolution summary for ticket TCK-2026-00200.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.ResolutionSummary FROM Tickets t WHERE t.TicketNumber='TCK-2026-00200'

Scenarios.Question: Find tickets containing 'printer' in the title.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t WHERE t.Title LIKE N'%printer%' ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Find tickets about 'data loss' or 'data corruption'.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t WHERE t.Title LIKE N'%data loss%' OR t.Title LIKE N'%data corruption%' OR t.Description LIKE N'%data loss%' OR t.Description LIKE N'%data corruption%' ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC
ExpectedFilters: Title or Description contains data loss/corruption

Scenarios.Question: Show tickets where the description mentions 'network'.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t WHERE t.Description LIKE N'%network%' ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Tickets whose title starts with 'Detailed'.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t WHERE t.Title LIKE N'Detailed%' ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Tickets containing 'login' or 'authentication' in the title.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t WHERE t.Title LIKE N'%login%' OR t.Title LIKE N'%authentication%' ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Find tickets mentioning 'VPN' in title or description.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t WHERE t.Title LIKE N'%VPN%' OR t.Description LIKE N'%VPN%' ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Tickets whose resolution mentions 'restart'.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.ResolutionSummary FROM Tickets t WHERE t.ResolutionSummary LIKE N'%restart%' ORDER BY t.ResolvedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Tickets where the technical assessment mentions 'memory leak'.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.TechnicalAssessment FROM Tickets t WHERE t.TechnicalAssessment LIKE N'%memory leak%' ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Show open critical tickets.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, s.Name AS StatusName, p.Name AS PriorityName FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON t.StatusId=s.Id JOIN TicketPriorities p ON t.PriorityId=p.Id WHERE s.Name='Open' AND p.Name='Critical' ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Tickets at High or Critical priority.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, p.Name AS PriorityName FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketPriorities p ON t.PriorityId=p.Id WHERE p.Name IN ('High','Critical') ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Tickets created between 2026-02-01 and 2026-03-31.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.CreatedAt FROM Tickets t WHERE t.CreatedAt BETWEEN '2026-02-01' AND '2026-03-31 23:59:59' ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Tickets in Health that are critical and unresolved.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t JOIN Entitys e ON t.EntityId=e.Id JOIN TicketPriorities p ON t.PriorityId=p.Id WHERE e.Name='Health' AND p.Name='Critical' AND t.ResolvedAt IS NULL ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Open tickets that breached the SLA.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON t.StatusId=s.Id WHERE s.Name='Open' AND t.IsSlaBreached=1 ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Tickets in Hardware or Network category.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, c.Name AS CategoryName FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketCategories c ON t.CategoryId=c.Id WHERE c.Name IN ('Hardware','Network') ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Critical tickets reported via Phone or Email.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketPriorities p ON t.PriorityId=p.Id JOIN TicketSources src ON t.SourceId=src.Id WHERE p.Name='Critical' AND src.Name IN ('Phone','Email') ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Open or In Progress tickets that require manager review.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON t.StatusId=s.Id WHERE s.Name IN ('Open','In Progress') AND t.RequiresManagerReview=1 ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Unresolved critical tickets with affected users greater than 5.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.AffectedUsersCount FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketPriorities p ON t.PriorityId=p.Id WHERE p.Name='Critical' AND t.ResolvedAt IS NULL AND t.AffectedUsersCount>5 ORDER BY t.AffectedUsersCount DESC

Scenarios.Question: Tickets in Education or Health created in the last 30 days.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, e.Name AS EntityName, t.CreatedAt FROM Tickets t JOIN Entitys e ON t.EntityId=e.Id WHERE e.Name IN ('Education','Health') AND t.CreatedAt>=DATEADD(day,-30,GETUTCDATE()) ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Resolved tickets with no comments.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t WHERE t.ResolvedAt IS NOT NULL AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM TicketComments c WHERE c.TicketId=t.Id) ORDER BY t.ResolvedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Pending tickets older than 14 days.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.CreatedAt FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON t.StatusId=s.Id WHERE s.Name='Pending' AND t.CreatedAt<DATEADD(day,-14,GETUTCDATE()) ORDER BY t.CreatedAt ASC

Scenarios.Question: Show ticket number, title, status, priority, entity, and category in one row.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, s.Name AS StatusName, p.Name AS PriorityName, e.Name AS EntityName, c.Name AS CategoryName FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON t.StatusId=s.Id JOIN TicketPriorities p ON t.PriorityId=p.Id JOIN Entitys e ON t.EntityId=e.Id JOIN TicketCategories c ON t.CategoryId=c.Id ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Show open critical tickets in Health with the assignee name.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, s.Name AS StatusName, p.Name AS PriorityName, e.Name AS EntityName, (u.FirstName+' '+u.LastName) AS AssignedToName FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON t.StatusId=s.Id JOIN TicketPriorities p ON t.PriorityId=p.Id JOIN Entitys e ON t.EntityId=e.Id LEFT JOIN AspNetUsers u ON t.AssignedToUserId=u.Id WHERE s.Name='Open' AND p.Name='Critical' AND e.Name='Health' ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Show ticket number with entity and source name together.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, e.Name AS EntityName, src.Name AS SourceName FROM Tickets t JOIN Entitys e ON t.EntityId=e.Id JOIN TicketSources src ON t.SourceId=src.Id ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Critical tickets with status, entity, category, and source names.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, s.Name AS StatusName, e.Name AS EntityName, c.Name AS CategoryName, src.Name AS SourceName FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON t.StatusId=s.Id JOIN TicketPriorities p ON t.PriorityId=p.Id JOIN Entitys e ON t.EntityId=e.Id JOIN TicketCategories c ON t.CategoryId=c.Id JOIN TicketSources src ON t.SourceId=src.Id WHERE p.Name='Critical' ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Show resolved Hardware tickets in Health with reporter and resolver names.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, e.Name AS EntityName, c.Name AS CategoryName, (rep.FirstName+' '+rep.LastName) AS ReporterName, (res.FirstName+' '+res.LastName) AS ResolvedByName FROM Tickets t JOIN Entitys e ON t.EntityId=e.Id JOIN TicketCategories c ON t.CategoryId=c.Id LEFT JOIN AspNetUsers rep ON t.CreatedByUserId=rep.Id LEFT JOIN AspNetUsers res ON t.ResolvedByUserId=res.Id WHERE c.Name='Hardware' AND e.Name='Health' AND t.ResolvedAt IS NOT NULL ORDER BY t.ResolvedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Tickets that have both attachments and comments.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM TicketAttachments a WHERE a.TicketId=t.Id) AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM TicketComments c WHERE c.TicketId=t.Id) ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Show ticket number, status, priority, and the most recent history action per ticket.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, s.Name AS StatusName, p.Name AS PriorityName, h.Action AS LastAction FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON t.StatusId=s.Id JOIN TicketPriorities p ON t.PriorityId=p.Id OUTER APPLY (SELECT TOP 1 hh.Action FROM TicketHistories hh WHERE hh.TicketId=t.Id ORDER BY hh.CreatedAt DESC) h ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Critical tickets with assignee, reporter, and approver in one row.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, (asg.FirstName+' '+asg.LastName) AS AssignedToName, (rep.FirstName+' '+rep.LastName) AS ReporterName, (apr.FirstName+' '+apr.LastName) AS ApprovedByName FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketPriorities p ON t.PriorityId=p.Id LEFT JOIN AspNetUsers asg ON t.AssignedToUserId=asg.Id LEFT JOIN AspNetUsers rep ON t.CreatedByUserId=rep.Id LEFT JOIN AspNetUsers apr ON t.ResolutionApprovedByUserId=apr.Id WHERE p.Name='Critical' ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: How many ticket comments are there in total?

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT COUNT_BIG(1) AS TotalComments FROM TicketComments

Scenarios.Question: Top 10 tickets by comment count.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 10 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, COUNT_BIG(c.Id) AS CommentCount FROM TicketComments c JOIN Tickets t ON c.TicketId=t.Id GROUP BY t.TicketNumber, t.Title ORDER BY CommentCount DESC

Scenarios.Question: How many attachments per ticket — top 5.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 5 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, COUNT_BIG(a.Id) AS AttachmentCount FROM TicketAttachments a JOIN Tickets t ON a.TicketId=t.Id GROUP BY t.TicketNumber, t.Title ORDER BY AttachmentCount DESC

Scenarios.Question: Show the 10 most recent ticket history changes.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 10 h.Action, h.OldValue, h.NewValue, t.TicketNumber, h.CreatedAt FROM TicketHistories h JOIN Tickets t ON h.TicketId=t.Id ORDER BY h.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: How many attachments are there in total?

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT COUNT_BIG(1) AS TotalAttachments FROM TicketAttachments

Scenarios.Question: Show the comments on ticket TCK-2026-00100.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 c.CreatedAt, c.Comment FROM TicketComments c JOIN Tickets t ON c.TicketId=t.Id WHERE t.TicketNumber='TCK-2026-00100' ORDER BY c.CreatedAt ASC

Scenarios.Question: Show attachments on ticket TCK-2026-00250.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 a.FileName, a.CreatedAt FROM TicketAttachments a JOIN Tickets t ON a.TicketId=t.Id WHERE t.TicketNumber='TCK-2026-00250' ORDER BY a.CreatedAt ASC

Scenarios.Question: Show the change history of ticket TCK-2026-00050.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT h.Action, h.OldValue, h.NewValue, h.CreatedAt FROM TicketHistories h JOIN Tickets t ON h.TicketId=t.Id WHERE t.TicketNumber='TCK-2026-00050' ORDER BY h.CreatedAt ASC

Scenarios.Question: Tickets with no comments at all.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM TicketComments c WHERE c.TicketId=t.Id) ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Tickets with no attachments.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM TicketAttachments a WHERE a.TicketId=t.Id) ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Average number of comments per ticket.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT AVG(CAST(CommentCount AS float)) AS AverageCommentsPerTicket FROM (SELECT TicketId, COUNT_BIG(1) AS CommentCount FROM TicketComments GROUP BY TicketId) x

Scenarios.Question: How many history entries exist per status change action?

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT h.Action, COUNT_BIG(1) AS EventCount FROM TicketHistories h GROUP BY h.Action ORDER BY EventCount DESC

Scenarios.Question: Show all roles.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 r.Name FROM AspNetRoles r ORDER BY r.Name

Scenarios.Question: Show all users with their email.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 u.FirstName, u.LastName, u.Email FROM AspNetUsers u ORDER BY u.LastName

Scenarios.Question: Show users in the Admin role.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 u.FirstName, u.LastName, u.Email FROM AspNetUsers u JOIN AspNetUserRoles ur ON u.Id=ur.UserId JOIN AspNetRoles r ON ur.RoleId=r.Id WHERE r.Name='Admin'

Scenarios.Question: Count of users per role.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT r.Name AS RoleName, COUNT_BIG(ur.UserId) AS UsersInRole FROM AspNetRoles r LEFT JOIN AspNetUserRoles ur ON r.Id=ur.RoleId GROUP BY r.Name ORDER BY UsersInRole DESC

Scenarios.Question: Top 5 users by number of assigned tickets.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 5 (u.FirstName+' '+u.LastName) AS AssignedToName, COUNT_BIG(t.Id) AS TicketCount FROM Tickets t JOIN AspNetUsers u ON t.AssignedToUserId=u.Id GROUP BY u.FirstName, u.LastName ORDER BY TicketCount DESC

Scenarios.Question: Users in the Tech entity.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 u.FirstName, u.LastName, u.Email FROM AspNetUsers u JOIN Entitys e ON u.EntityId=e.Id WHERE e.Name LIKE '%Technologies%' ORDER BY u.LastName

Scenarios.Question: Top 3 resolvers by number of resolved tickets.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 3 (u.FirstName+' '+u.LastName) AS ResolvedByName, COUNT_BIG(t.Id) AS ResolvedCount FROM Tickets t JOIN AspNetUsers u ON t.ResolvedByUserId=u.Id WHERE t.ResolvedAt IS NOT NULL GROUP BY u.FirstName, u.LastName ORDER BY ResolvedCount DESC

Scenarios.Question: How many users do not belong to any role?

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT COUNT_BIG(1) AS RolelessUsers FROM AspNetUsers u WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM AspNetUserRoles ur WHERE ur.UserId=u.Id)

Scenarios.Question: Find tickets similar to TCK-2026-00100.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: EXEC sp_FindSimilarTickets @TicketNumber='TCK-2026-00100'

Scenarios.Question: Show me tickets that look like TCK-2026-00050.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: EXEC sp_FindSimilarTickets @TicketNumber='TCK-2026-00050'

Scenarios.Question: Are there any past tickets similar to a printer-driver issue?

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 10 t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t WHERE t.Title LIKE N'%printer%' OR t.Description LIKE N'%printer%' ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Find similar tickets to a VPN connection drop problem.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 10 t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t WHERE t.Title LIKE N'%VPN%' OR t.Description LIKE N'%VPN%' ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Look up tickets related to TCK-2026-00200.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: EXEC sp_FindSimilarTickets @TicketNumber='TCK-2026-00200'

Scenarios.Question: What other tickets are related to TCK-2026-00010?

CorrectAnswer.SQL: EXEC sp_FindSimilarTickets @TicketNumber='TCK-2026-00010'

Scenarios.Question: What is the SOP for handling a critical ticket escalation?
How do I troubleshoot an integration failure?
When does a ticket require manager review?
Walk me through the resolution approval workflow.
What's the difference between Pending and Closed status?
Explain how SLA breach is determined.
Assign ticket TCK-2026-00100 to user agent1@tech.local.
Set the status of ticket TCK-2026-00100 to Resolved.
Add a comment to ticket TCK-2026-00050 saying 'investigating now'.
Escalate ticket TCK-2026-00010 to level 2.
Change the priority of ticket TCK-2026-00200 to Critical.
Resolve ticket TCK-2026-00500 with summary 'restarted service'.
What's the weather in Riyadh today?
Get me the current weather for Dubai.
Will it rain in Cairo tomorrow?
Is it cold in Amman right now?
Hi.
Who are you and what can you help me with?
Thanks, that was useful.
What kinds of questions can I ask you about tickets?
Show details of ticket 99999.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT t.* FROM Tickets t WHERE t.Id=99999

Scenarios.Question: Tickets in entity Mars Colony.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT t.* FROM Tickets t JOIN Entitys e ON t.EntityId=e.Id WHERE e.Name='Mars Colony'

Scenarios.Question: Tickets with priority Catastrophic.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT t.* FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketPriorities p ON t.PriorityId=p.Id WHERE p.Name='Catastrophic'

Scenarios.Question: Show ticket TCK-9999-99999.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT t.* FROM Tickets t WHERE t.TicketNumber='TCK-9999-99999'

Scenarios.Question: Tickets in category Telepathic Diagnostics.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT t.* FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketCategories c ON t.CategoryId=c.Id WHERE c.Name='Telepathic Diagnostics'

Scenarios.Question: Show users in role Overlord.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT u.Email FROM AspNetUsers u JOIN AspNetUserRoles ur ON u.Id=ur.UserId JOIN AspNetRoles r ON ur.RoleId=r.Id WHERE r.Name='Overlord'

Scenarios.Question: What's a good recipe for biryani?
Tell me a joke.
What is the capital of Japan?
Translate 'good morning' to Spanish.
Solve x^2 + 2x - 3 = 0.
Write a haiku about debugging.
How tall is Mount Everest?
Who won the 2024 Champions League?
What's the meaning of life?
Sing me a song about cats.
How many tickets are there in total?

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT COUNT_BIG(1) AS TotalTickets FROM Tickets

Scenarios.Question: Show 10 critical tickets sorted by creation date.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 10 t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketPriorities p ON t.PriorityId=p.Id WHERE p.Name='Critical' ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Tickets in the Hardware category.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketCategories c ON t.CategoryId=c.Id WHERE c.Name='Hardware' ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Show the comments on ticket TCK-2026-00077.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 c.CreatedAt, c.Comment FROM TicketComments c JOIN Tickets t ON c.TicketId=t.Id WHERE t.TicketNumber='TCK-2026-00077' ORDER BY c.CreatedAt ASC

Scenarios.Question: Tickets created in the last 7 days.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.CreatedAt FROM Tickets t WHERE t.CreatedAt>=DATEADD(day,-7,GETUTCDATE()) ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Open critical tickets with the assignee name.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, (u.FirstName+' '+u.LastName) AS AssignedToName FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON t.StatusId=s.Id JOIN TicketPriorities p ON t.PriorityId=p.Id LEFT JOIN AspNetUsers u ON t.AssignedToUserId=u.Id WHERE s.Name='Open' AND p.Name='Critical' ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Find tickets containing 'printer' in the title.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t WHERE t.Title LIKE N'%printer%' ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Average affected users per ticket.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT AVG(CAST(AffectedUsersCount AS float)) AS AverageAffectedUsers FROM Tickets

Scenarios.Question: Show all roles.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT TOP 50 r.Name FROM AspNetRoles r ORDER BY r.Name

Scenarios.Question: Entities with more than 100 tickets.

CorrectAnswer.SQL: SELECT e.Name AS EntityName, COUNT_BIG(1) AS TicketCount FROM Tickets t JOIN Entitys e ON t.EntityId=e.Id GROUP BY e.Name HAVING COUNT_BIG(1)>100 ORDER BY TicketCount DESC
